azurefox devops

├──────────────┼────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│prod-platform │deploy-aks-prod         │azure-repos:platform-api; visibility=visible; refs/heads/main        │auto-trigger; triggers=continuousIntegration│repo-content (read)            │connections=prod-subscription; auth=ServicePrincipal           │secret-vars=8; groups=2; keyvault=prod-kv-release│AKS/Kubernetes, ACR/Containers          │Check aks for the named deployment target. Review permissions and role-trusts for Azure control; review keyvault for vault-backed support.            │Build definition 'deploy-aks-prod' in project 'prod-platform' exposes an Azure change path. trusted inputs include repository azure-repos:platform-api@refs/heads/main. execution can start through auto-trigger. current credentials can read repository azure-repos:platform-api@refs/heads/main, but not write it from Azure DevOps evidence here. uses Azure-facing service connection(s) prod-subscription. references variable group(s) prod-release-secrets, prod-kv-release. surfaces 8 secret-marked variable name(s). pulls from Key Vault-backed variable support (kv-prod-shared). source clues ground likely Azure impact in AKS/Kubernetes, ACR/Containers. This path trusts repository azure-repos:platform-api@refs/heads/main, and current credentials can read it but not write it from Azure DevOps evidence here. Check aks for the named deployment target; review permissions and role-trusts for Azure control; review keyvault for the vault-backed support.│
│prod-platform │deploy-appservice-prod  │azure-repos:customer-portal; visibility=visible; refs/heads/main     │pr-trigger; triggers=pullRequest            │repo-content, pull-request     │connections=prod-appsvc-wif; auth=WorkloadIdentityFederation   │secret-vars=3; groups=1                          │App Service, App Service: app-public-api│Check app-services for the named deployment target. Review permissions and role-trusts for Azure control.                                             │Build definition 'deploy-appservice-prod' in project 'prod-platform' exposes an Azure change path. trusted inputs include repository azure-repos:customer-portal@refs/heads/main. execution can start through pr-trigger. current credentials can poison repo-content through repository azure-repos:customer-portal@refs/heads/main. uses Azure-facing service connection(s) prod-appsvc-wif. references variable group(s) prod-appsvc-release. surfaces 3 secret-marked variable name(s). source clues ground likely Azure impact in App Service. Current credentials can poison repo-content through repository azure-repos:customer-portal@refs/heads/main. Check app-services for the named deployment target; review permissions and role-trusts for Azure control.                                                                                                                                                                                                           │
│prod-platform │deploy-artifact-app-prod│azure-repos:release-orchestrator; visibility=visible; refs/heads/main│artifact-trigger; triggers=buildCompletion  │pipeline-artifact (exists-only)│connections=prod-artifacts-wif; auth=WorkloadIdentityFederation│secret-vars=2; groups=1                          │App Service, ACR/Containers             │Check app-services for the named deployment target. Review permissions and role-trusts for Azure control.                                             │Build definition 'deploy-artifact-app-prod' in project 'prod-platform' exposes an Azure change path. trusted inputs include pipeline artifact prod-platform/shared-build#signed-drop, template repository azure-repos:platform-templates@refs/heads/main, 5 additional trusted input(s). execution can start through artifact-trigger. Azure DevOps evidence currently only proves that pipeline artifact prod-platform/shared-build#signed-drop is trusted here. uses Azure-facing service connection(s) prod-artifacts-wif. references variable group(s) prod-artifact-release. surfaces 2 secret-marked variable name(s). source clues ground likely Azure impact in App Service, ACR/Containers. This path trusts pipeline artifact prod-platform/shared-build#signed-drop, but current evidence only shows that it exists. Check app-services for the named deployment target; review permissions and role-trusts for Azure control.                                           │
│platform-infra│plan-infra-prod         │github:infra-live; visibility=external-reference; refs/heads/main    │schedule; triggers=schedule                 │repo-content (exists-only)     │connections=infra-subscription; auth=ManagedServiceIdentity    │groups=1; keyvault=platform-kv                   │ARM/Bicep/Terraform                     │Check arm-deployments for the named deployment target. Review permissions and role-trusts for Azure control; review keyvault for vault-backed support.│Build definition 'plan-infra-prod' in project 'platform-infra' exposes an Azure change path. trusted inputs include repository github:infra-live@refs/heads/main. execution can start through schedule. Azure DevOps evidence currently only proves that repository github:infra-live@refs/heads/main is trusted here. uses Azure-facing service connection(s) infra-subscription. references variable group(s) platform-kv. pulls from Key Vault-backed variable support (kv-platform-shared). source clues ground likely Azure impact in ARM/Bicep/Terraform. This path trusts repository github:infra-live@refs/heads/main, but current evidence only shows that it exists. Check arm-deployments for the named deployment target; review permissions and role-trusts for Azure control; review keyvault for the vault-backed support.                                                                                                                                           │
╰──────────────┴────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

Takeaway: 4 Azure DevOps build definition(s) surfaced; 1 expose a proven current-credential injection point, 1 add queue-only support without poisoning proof, 3 point to visible Azure Repos sources, 1 point to external sources, 1 trust non-repo inputs, and 4 show Azure-facing service connections.
